interfaces.name: Ticker
interfaces.doc:
A stateful ticker that fires registered listeners with an incrementing
counter. `on_tick` is a `Shape::Subscription` op — it REGISTERS a host
callback (a PHP `callable`/`Closure`) and hands back an owning `Subscription`
handle whose unsubscribe() removes the listener; `tick` fires every live
listener SYNCHRONOUSLY on the PHP request thread. This is the callback +
subscription slice's PHP (ext-php-rs) round-trip proof. PHP callbacks are
documented SYNC-ONLY: the callable is only ever invoked on the request thread
that supplied it (off-thread invocation is unsupported) — see the PhpCb marker.

ops.name: new
ops.doc: Open a fresh ticker with no listeners and the counter at 0.

ops.name: on_tick
ops.doc:
Register `listener` to be called on every `tick`; returns a Subscription
handle that removes the listener when unsubscribed. The listener is invoked
synchronously on the PHP request thread (the sync-only contract).

ops.name: tick
ops.doc:
Fire every live listener with the current counter value, then increment it
(infallible — registration/firing never fails).
